title: First integrals for non linear hyperbolic equations
comment:
38 pages, 1 figure, v2: some sign conventions have been changed, some
  minor corrections has been added
summary:
Given a solution of a nonlinear wave equation on the flat space-time (with a
real analytic nonlinearity), we relate its Cauchy data at two different times
by nonlinear representation formulas in terms of asymptotic series. We first
show how to construct formally these series by mean of generating functions
based on an algebraic framework inspired by the construction of Fock spaces in
quantum field theory. Then we build an analytic setting in which all these
constructions really make sense and give rise to convergent series.

title: Gradient NLW on curved background in 4+1 dimensions
summary:
We obtain a sharp local well-posedness result for the Gradient
  Nonlinear Wave Equation on a nonsmooth curved background. In the process we
introduce variable coefficient versions of Bourgain's $X^{s,b}$ spaces, and use
a trilinear multiscale wave packet decomposition in order to prove a key
trilinear estimate.
